spanish.phrase: No hay mal que por bien no venga.
spanish.note: Every cloud has a silver lining — but more fatalistic.

spanish.phrase: El que mucho abarca, poco aprieta.
spanish.note: Jack of all trades, master of none.

spanish.phrase: A palabras necias, oídos sordos.
spanish.note: Don't dignify foolishness with a response.

spanish.phrase: Más sabe el diablo por viejo que por diablo.
spanish.note: Experience trumps innate ability.

spanish.phrase: En boca cerrada no entran moscas.
spanish.note: Silence protects you from trouble.

spanish.phrase: No hay peor ciego que el que no quiere ver.
spanish.note: Willful ignorance is the worst kind.

spanish.phrase: Cría cuervos y te sacarán los ojos.
spanish.note: Ingratitude from those you've helped.

spanish.phrase: El que nace para tamal, del cielo le caen las hojas.
spanish.note: Fatalistic — some are destined for certain paths.

spanish.phrase: Agua que no has de beber, déjala correr.
spanish.note: Don't meddle in what doesn't concern you.

spanish.phrase: A falta de pan, tortillas.
spanish.note: Make do with what you have.

spanish.phrase: Del dicho al hecho hay mucho trecho.
spanish.note: Actions speak louder than words.

spanish.phrase: No hay quinto malo.
spanish.note: The fifth drink is never a bad idea (ironic drinking culture reference).

spanish.phrase: El que con lobos anda, a aullar se enseña.
spanish.note: You become like the company you keep.

spanish.phrase: Cuando el río suena, agua lleva.
spanish.note: Where there's smoke, there's fire.

spanish.phrase: Camarón que se duerme, se lo lleva la corriente.
spanish.note: Stay alert or get left behind.

spanish.phrase: Al mal tiempo, buena cara.
spanish.note: Keep your chin up in hard times.

spanish.phrase: El que es perico, donde quiera es verde.
spanish.note: Talent shows regardless of circumstance.

spanish.phrase: Más vale tarde que nunca, pero más vale nunca tarde.
spanish.note: A twist on the classic — punctuality matters.

spanish.phrase: Lo que no te mata, te fortalece... o te deja traumado.
spanish.note: Modern dark humor spin on Nietzsche.

spanish.phrase: El flojo trabaja doble.
spanish.note: Cutting corners creates more work.

spanish.phrase: Dime con quién andas y te diré quién eres.
spanish.note: You're judged by the company you keep.

spanish.phrase: El que no transa, no avanza.
spanish.note: Cynical commentary on corruption — 'if you don't cheat, you don't advance.'

spanish.phrase: Ahogarse en un vaso de agua.
spanish.note: To make a mountain out of a molehill.

spanish.phrase: Estar entre la espada y la pared.
spanish.note: Between a rock and a hard place.

spanish.phrase: No tiene ni pies ni cabeza.
spanish.note: It makes no sense whatsoever.

spanish.phrase: Meter la pata.
spanish.note: To screw up, put your foot in your mouth.

spanish.phrase: Tomar el pelo.
spanish.note: To pull someone's leg, mess with them.

spanish.phrase: Ser pan comido.
spanish.note: To be a piece of cake.

spanish.phrase: Estar en las nubes.
spanish.note: To be distracted, head in the clouds.

spanish.phrase: Tirar la toalla.
spanish.note: To throw in the towel.

spanish.phrase: Dorar la píldora.
spanish.note: To sugarcoat something unpleasant.

spanish.phrase: No tener pelos en la lengua.
spanish.note: To speak bluntly, not mince words.

spanish.phrase: Quedarse con los brazos cruzados.
spanish.note: To stand idly by, do nothing.

spanish.phrase: Dar en el clavo.
spanish.note: To hit the nail on the head.

spanish.phrase: Buscarle tres pies al gato.
spanish.note: To overcomplicate things, look for trouble.

spanish.phrase: Echarle mucha crema a los tacos.
spanish.note: To exaggerate, brag excessively.

spanish.phrase: Hacerse de la vista gorda.
spanish.note: To turn a blind eye.

spanish.phrase: Ser uña y carne.
spanish.note: To be inseparable, thick as thieves.

spanish.phrase: Ponerse las pilas.
spanish.note: To get one's act together, focus up.

spanish.phrase: No hay tu tía.
spanish.note: There's no way around it, no alternative.

spanish.phrase: Estar como agua para chocolate.
spanish.note: To be boiling with rage (or passion).

spanish.phrase: Ya ni la amuela.
spanish.note: That's the last straw / unbelievable.

spanish.phrase: Chingar es un verbo que se conjuga en todos los tiempos.
spanish.note: Meta-commentary on Mexico's most versatile word.

spanish.phrase: El que nada debe, nada teme.
spanish.note: A clear conscience fears nothing.

spanish.phrase: A otro perro con ese hueso.
spanish.note: Tell that to someone who'll believe it.

spanish.phrase: Dar gato por liebre.
spanish.note: To deceive, pass off something inferior.

spanish.phrase: Salir el tiro por la culata.
spanish.note: For a plan to backfire.

spanish.phrase: Más vale maña que fuerza.
spanish.note: Brain over brawn.

spanish.phrase: El que tiene tienda, que la atienda.
spanish.note: Take care of your responsibilities.

spanish.phrase: Éramos muchos y parió la abuela.
spanish.note: Just when we thought it couldn't get worse.

spanish.phrase: Cada cabeza es un mundo.
spanish.note: Everyone sees things differently.

spanish.phrase: De tal palo, tal astilla.
spanish.note: Like father, like son.

spanish.phrase: El que mucho se despide, pocas ganas tiene de irse.
spanish.note: Long goodbyes reveal reluctance to leave.

spanish.phrase: En casa del herrero, cuchillo de palo.
spanish.note: The cobbler's children have no shoes.

spanish.phrase: La curiosidad mató al gato, pero murió sabiendo.
spanish.note: Modern twist — at least the cat learned something.

spanish.phrase: Más vale solo que mal acompañado.
spanish.note: Better alone than in bad company.

spanish.phrase: Perro que ladra no muerde.
spanish.note: All bark and no bite.

spanish.phrase: Quien siembra vientos, cosecha tempestades.
spanish.note: You reap what you sow.

spanish.phrase: A rey muerto, rey puesto.
spanish.note: Everyone is replaceable.

spanish.phrase: Mucho ruido y pocas nueces.
spanish.note: Much ado about nothing.

spanish.phrase: Las cuentas claras y el chocolate espeso.
spanish.note: Keep finances clear between friends.

spanish.phrase: Al pan, pan, y al vino, vino.
spanish.note: Call a spade a spade.

spanish.phrase: Ni tanto que queme al santo, ni tanto que no lo alumbre.
spanish.note: Everything in moderation.

spanish.phrase: El que paga, manda.
spanish.note: He who pays the piper calls the tune.

spanish.phrase: Ojos que no ven, corazón que no siente.
spanish.note: Out of sight, out of mind.

spanish.phrase: Lo barato sale caro.
spanish.note: You get what you pay for.

spanish.phrase: A caballo regalado no se le miran los dientes.
spanish.note: Don't look a gift horse in the mouth.

spanish.phrase: El tiempo pone a cada quien en su lugar.
spanish.note: Time reveals everyone's true character.

spanish.phrase: No hay fecha que no se llegue ni plazo que no se cumpla.
spanish.note: Every deadline arrives eventually.

spanish.phrase: Una golondrina no hace verano.
spanish.note: One swallow doesn't make a summer — don't jump to conclusions.

spanish.phrase: Después de la tormenta viene la calma.
spanish.note: The storm always passes.

spanish.phrase: No dejes para mañana lo que puedes hacer hoy.
spanish.note: Don't procrastinate.

spanish.phrase: Haz bien y no mires a quién.
spanish.note: Do good without expecting recognition.

spanish.phrase: El mundo es un pañuelo.
spanish.note: It's a small world.

spanish.phrase: Entre broma y broma, la verdad se asoma.
spanish.note: Many a true word is spoken in jest.

spanish.phrase: El muerto al pozo y el vivo al gozo.
spanish.note: Life goes on — darkly pragmatic.

spanish.phrase: La vida no es como la pintan.
spanish.note: Life isn't what they make it out to be.

spanish.phrase: Lo que bien se aprende, nunca se olvida.
spanish.note: What's learned well is never forgotten.

spanish.phrase: De noche todos los gatos son pardos.
spanish.note: In darkness, distinctions disappear — used for moral ambiguity.

spanish.phrase: Candil de la calle, oscuridad de su casa.
spanish.note: Generous outside, neglectful at home.

spanish.phrase: El hábito no hace al monje.
spanish.note: Don't judge by appearances.

spanish.phrase: Hierba mala nunca muere.
spanish.note: Bad weeds never die — often said self-deprecatingly.

spanish.phrase: La esperanza es lo último que se pierde.
spanish.note: Hope dies last.

spanish.phrase: Sobre gustos no hay nada escrito.
spanish.note: There's no accounting for taste.

spanish.phrase: El amor entra por la cocina.
spanish.note: The way to the heart is through the stomach.

spanish.phrase: Cuentas claras, amistades largas.
spanish.note: Clear accounts make lasting friendships.

spanish.phrase: Más vale prevenir que lamentar.
spanish.note: Better safe than sorry.

spanish.phrase: La práctica hace al maestro.
spanish.note: Practice makes perfect.

spanish.phrase: A quien madruga, Dios le ayuda.
spanish.note: The early bird catches the worm.

spanish.phrase: No es lo mismo hablar de toros que estar en el ruedo.
spanish.note: It's easy to talk, harder to do.

spanish.phrase: La letra con sangre entra.
spanish.note: Old saying about harsh discipline in learning — now used critically.

spanish.phrase: A lo hecho, pecho.
spanish.note: Face the consequences of your actions.

spanish.phrase: Dios los cría y ellos se juntan.
spanish.note: Birds of a feather flock together.

spanish.phrase: El que ríe al último, ríe mejor.
spanish.note: He who laughs last, laughs best.

spanish.phrase: Más vale pájaro en mano que cien volando.
spanish.note: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.

spanish.phrase: Genio y figura hasta la sepultura.
spanish.note: A leopard can't change its spots.

spanish.phrase: No hay rosas sin espinas.
spanish.note: Every rose has its thorn.

italian.phrase: Sono di Chicago, ma abito in Messico.
A che ora apre il museo?
Che cosa fai nel tempo libero?
Suono la chitarra da tre anni.
Vorrei prenotare un tavolo per due.
A che ora devo lasciare la camera?
Vorrei un biglietto di andata e ritorno.
Da quale binario parte il treno?
Sono in ritardo, arrivo tra cinque minuti.
È la prima volta che vengo in Italia.
Qual è il tuo piatto preferito?
Il mio compleanno è in maggio.
